SHS Magalang Stand Alone II — Growth Opportunities
🏠 Home › Growth Opportunities
A strength-based, forward-looking school development perspective
A
Admin ▾
🌱
How We Read This Data — A Note for the School Community The five-year data at SHS Magalang Stand Alone II tells a story of a school that has continued to serve its students with care and dedication through a challenging period. The current low dropout rate, the growing faculty, and the available physical resources are not accidents — they reflect the commitment of the entire school community.
🏅 What the Data Says the School is Already Doing Well
✓ Achievement
Lowest Dropout Rate in 5 Years (2.30%)
The current 2.30% dropout rate is the best in the school's recent history — and it is below the DepEd national SHS target of 3%. This is a meaningful, data-confirmed achievement that reflects how well the school's teachers and support staff care for their students.
✓ Achievement
Enrollment Decline is Slowing Down Significantly
The rate of enrollment decline dropped sharply from −20.26% (2024–25) to −8.00% (2025–26). This deceleration means the school's retention efforts are working — students are staying, even in a more competitive environment.
✓ Resource Ready
Available Space for New Programs
At 70% classroom utilization, the school has physical space to launch new student support programs, remediation centers, TVL labs, or even open its campus to community-based learning — all without any new construction needed.
✓ Resource Ready
Dedicated Faculty with Personalized Teaching Capacity
Faculty count grew from 23 to 27 even during enrollment challenges. The resulting 14:1 student-teacher ratio means students receive more individualized instruction than most — a genuine quality advantage.
🚀 Growth Opportunities — Turning Strengths into Strategy
📣 Branding
The "Small School, Bigger Attention" Advantage
With a 14:1 student-teacher ratio and available classroom space, Stand Alone II offers something most larger schools cannot: genuine individual attention.
→ Develop outreach materials highlighting personalized learning
🤝 Industry
TVL as a Direct Local Livelihood Pathway
With 4–5 dedicated TVL teachers and available lab space, the school is ready to formalize employer partnerships that turn the TVL strand into a visible pathway to local employment.
→ Establish MOAs with local businesses for immersion and placement
📚 Student Support
Turn Spare Rooms into Student Success Spaces
Repurposing underutilized rooms as a remediation suite, peer tutoring area, or student wellness space creates visible proof that the school invests in its learners.
→ Designate 1–2 rooms for dedicated student support services
🔗 Community
Year-Round JHS Relationship Program
Structured, ongoing engagement with Grade 9 and 10 students in feeder JHS schools — through career talks, campus visits, and alumni mentoring — can rebuild the intake pipeline.
→ Assign a School-Community Relations role for feeder school visits
📊 Planning
Use This Dashboard for Budget & Policy Advocacy
The school's ability to present evidence-based, data-driven analysis is itself a strength. Sharing this dashboard with the DepEd Division, LGU, and school stakeholders builds credibility.
→ Present this analytics report at the next stakeholders' meeting
🌟 Culture
Celebrate & Communicate the Dropout Rate Win
Reaching a 5-year low dropout rate of 2.30% — below the national standard — is an achievement worth naming publicly. Recognizing teachers, guidance staff, and students builds school pride.
→ Feature this milestone in school events, community boards, and social media

SHS Magalang Stand Alone II — KPI Guide
🏠 Home › KPI Guide
Definitions, benchmarks, and how to interpret each metric
A
Admin ▾
👥Total Enrollment
The sum of all enrolled students in Grade 11 and Grade 12. Primary indicator of school size and community reach.
Total = G11 Enrollment + G12 Enrollment
Target: Stable or GrowingAlert: Declining YoY
📉YoY Growth Rate
Year-over-year percent change in total enrollment. Positive = growth, negative = decline. Measures speed and direction of enrollment momentum.
YoY% = ((Current − Previous) / Previous) × 100
Target: ≥ 0%Alert: < −10%
🚪Dropout Rate
Percentage of students who left school before completing the year. DepEd target for SHS is below 3%.
Dropout Rate = (Dropouts / Total Enrolled) × 100
Target: < 3%Alert: ≥ 5%
🔁Repeater Rate
Proportion of students repeating a grade level. High rates indicate academic difficulty or support gaps. Target: below 3%.
Repeater Rate = (Repeaters / Total Enrolled) × 100
Target: < 3%Alert: ≥ 5%
👩‍🏫Student-Teacher Ratio
Average number of students per teacher. Optimal SHS range is 18–25:1. Too low = teacher surplus; too high = overloaded teachers.
STR = Total Enrolled / Number of Teachers
Optimal: 18–25:1Alert: < 15:1
🏫Classroom Utilization Rate
How efficiently classrooms are being used against 14 classrooms × standard capacity. Reflects whether physical resources match the student population.
Util. = (Enrolled / (Rooms × Capacity)) × 100
Optimal: 75–100%Alert: > 110% or < 60%
⚠️At-Risk Count
Combined count of students who dropped out or are repeating a grade. Identifies the total number of students needing immediate support or intervention.
At-Risk = Dropouts + Repeaters
Target: Below 30Alert: Above 50
📊Grade 11 Intake
Number of new incoming Grade 11 students per year. The most forward-looking metric — declining G11 intake predicts future total enrollment decline before it fully materializes.
Intake = Raw G11 Enrollment per SY
Healthy: ≥ Prior Year G11Alert: Consecutive YoY drops
SHS Magalang Stand Alone II — KPI Formulas
🏠 Home › KPI Formulas
Complete formula reference with interpretation guide
A
Admin ▾
📐 All KPI Formulas
KPI Formula
Total EnrollmentG11 + G12
YoY Growth Rate((Curr − Prev) / Prev) × 100
Dropout Rate(Dropouts / Total) × 100
Repeater Rate(Repeaters / Total) × 100
Student-Teacher RatioTotal / Teachers
Classroom Util. Rate(Total / (Rooms × 49)) × 100
At-Risk CountDropouts + Repeaters
5-Year Net ChangeCurrent − Baseline (2021)
📏 Benchmark Legend
Green — On target, performing well
Gold — KPI optimal, no action needed
Orange — Approaching threshold, monitor
Red — Critical, intervention required
Blue — Informational, no immediate concern
🎓 DepEd Standard Targets (SHS)
Dropout RateBelow 3%
Repeater RateBelow 3%
Classroom Utilization75%–100%
Student-Teacher Ratio18–30:1
Enrollment GrowthStable or Increasing
SHS Magalang Stand Alone II — Methodology
🏠 Home › Methodology
Data collection, analysis framework, and interpretation guide
A
Admin ▾
School Data Analysis Methodology
Comprehensive framework for data collection, analysis, and interpretation
📋 1. Data Collection Framework
Time Period Coverage
Five-year comprehensive analysis spanning School Years 2021–2022 through 2025–2026, capturing enrollment trends, resource utilization, and student performance indicators.
Data Sources
• Official DepEd school records and enrollment documents
• School information system (SIS) for per-section breakdown
• School administrative reports (BOIE, DepEd forms)
• Classroom and resource inventory audit
• Faculty records and teacher allocation sheets
Key Indicators Collected
Enrollment Grade 11 & 12 totals, strand breakdown
Performance Dropout count, repeater count by grade
Resources Teacher count by subject, classroom count, seating capacity
Strands ABM, HUMSS/Academic, TVL enrollments & sections
🔢 2. Calculation Methods & Formulas
Year-over-Year (YoY) Enrollment Change
YoY % = ((Current Year Total – Prior Year Total) / Prior Year Total) × 100
Example: (391 – 425) ÷ 425 × 100 = –8.00%
Dropout Rate & Repeater Rate
Rate (%) = (Count ÷ Total Enrollment) × 100
Example: (9 ÷ 391) × 100 = 2.30% dropout rate
Student-Teacher Ratio (STR)
STR = Total Enrollment ÷ Number of Teachers
Example: 391 ÷ 27 = 14:1
Classroom Utilization Rate
Utilization % = (Total Enrollment ÷ Total Seating Capacity) × 100
Example: (391 ÷ 559) × 100 = 70%
📊 3. Data Analysis Techniques
Trend Analysis
Linear trend assessment over the 5-year period to identify consistent patterns. Enrollment decline measured from 669 (2021) → 391 (2026), revealing a 41.5% contraction. Year-specific turning points identified (critical year: 2024–2025 with –20.26% drop).
Comparative Assessment
Grade 11 vs. Grade 12 enrollment dynamics analyzed separately to isolate intake problems from retention issues. Strand breakdowns (ABM, HUMSS, TVL) tracked to identify shifting student preferences.
Efficiency Ratios
Resource utilization benchmarked against DepEd standards. Classroom utilization shifted from 120% (overcrowded) to 70% (high surplus). STR normalized from 29:1 to 14:1, revealing staffing imbalance.
Correlation Analysis
Examined relationship between dropout spikes and repeater counts (SY 2022–2023: 21 repeaters + 20 dropouts). Academic difficulty linked to student attrition. Grade 11 intake collapse identified as primary driver of total decline.
📈 4. Predictive Modeling & Forecasting
Linear Regression Approach
Based on 5-year historical decline (average 55–70 students/year), next two school years projected:
• SY 2026–2027: 336–331 students
• SY 2027–2028: 281–266 students
Assumption: No major policy or external factor changes
Three Scenario Planning
Best Case: Stabilization at ~420 (improved recruitment + retention)
Expected Case: Follows trend → ~360 (current pattern continues)
Worst Case: Acceleration → ~320 (compounded challenges)
Resource Projection
• Classroom needs: 5–6 sections (G11), 5–6 sections (G12) by 2027–28
• Teacher needs: No additional hiring; possible load reduction needed
• Utilization outlook: Further facility underutilization without intervention
✅ 5. Data Quality & Validation Measures
✓ Data Accuracy
All figures sourced from official DepEd school records. Cross-verified against administrative documents and annual reports.
✓ Temporal Consistency
5-year continuous series ensures year-over-year comparability. Consistent methodology applied across all years for calculation of rates and ratios.
⚠ Data Limitations
• Dropout classification may vary in how it's recorded (system absence vs. formal withdrawal)
• External factors (e.g., pandemic recovery, regional competition) not fully quantified
ℹ Benchmarking Standards
DepEd national targets applied: Dropout <3%, Repeater <3%, STR 18–30:1, Classroom Utilization 75–100%
💡 6. Dashboard Interpretation Guide
🟢 Positive Trend
On target, performing well
🟡 Gold / Optimal
KPI on target, no action needed
🟠 Moderate Concern
Approaching threshold, monitor
🔴 Critical
Needs immediate intervention
How to Read Trends: Arrow ↓ indicates decline; Arrow ↑ indicates growth. Magnitude of change shown in percentage. Multi-year trend lines in charts show sustained pattern vs. one-year anomaly.
📌 7. Recommendations for Data Use
For School Administrators:
Use enrollment forecasts to make staffing and resource allocation decisions. Quarterly monitor KPIs (dropout rate, STR, utilization) against DepEd benchmarks. Adjust interventions based on at-risk student lists.
For Policymakers & Division:
Allocate additional support to schools showing critical trends. Consider clustering or consolidation if trends persist. Fund targeted programs (bridge, tutoring, counseling) based on documented root causes.
For Teachers & Counselors:
Refer at-risk students to intervention programs. Track individual progress. Report back on intervention effectiveness so dashboard can be refined for next cycle.
Annual Review Cycle:
Dashboard updated each end of school year with finalized data. Trends re-analyzed. New forecasts generated. Recommendations revised based on intervention outcomes.
SHS Magalang Stand Alone II — User Guide
🏠 Home › User Guide
Step-by-step guide to every feature of the SHS Magalang Analytics Dashboard
A
Admin ▾
📖
USER GUIDE · SHS MAGALANG AIS V1.0
Welcome to the SHS Magalang Dashboard
This guide walks you through every feature of the system — from logging in to reading predictions. Follow each section in order so you can use the dashboard with confidence.
📊 Dashboard 🗂️ Raw Data 💡 Insights 🎯 Recs 🔬 Tools 🔐 Security
📋 Contents
Getting Started
1 Logging In
Main Modules
2 Dashboard 3 Raw Data 4 Insights 5 Recommendations 6 Growth Opps
Advanced Tools
7 Forecaster 8 Allocator 9 Interventions 10 Simulator
Account & Admin
11 Security 12 Tips & FAQ
🔑
Getting Started — Logging In
How to access the SHS Magalang Dashboard
1
Open the Dashboard
Go to the dashboard URL on your browser. You will be greeted by the login screen with the SHS Magalang logo and maroon theme.
2
Enter Your Credentials
Type your assigned username and password in the login form. Use the 👁 eye icon to toggle password visibility. Click Sign In to proceed.
3
You're In!
After a successful login you will land on the Dashboard overview page. The sidebar on the left and top navigation header are now accessible.
💡 Tip: Only authorized DepEd personnel with valid credentials can log in. Contact your school ICT coordinator if you cannot access the system.
📊
Module 1 — Dashboard
The main overview of school enrollment health
The Dashboard is your starting point. It shows a live snapshot of SHS Magalang's enrollment health across all tracked school years, from S.Y. 2020–2021 to S.Y. 2025–2026.
1
KPI Summary Cards (Top Row)
The top row shows 6 key performance indicators: Total Enrollment, YoY Growth, Dropout Rate, Repeater Rate, Student-Teacher Ratio, and Classroom Utilization. Each card shows the current value and a trend badge (↑ / ↓). Green = healthy, red = alert.
2
Enrollment Trend Chart
A line chart plots total enrollment, Grade 11, and Grade 12 across all school years. Hover over any point to see exact values. Use this to spot multi-year trends at a glance.
3
Year-by-Year Summary Table
A table below the chart lists all school years with enrollment, repeaters, dropouts, teachers, and classrooms. Click "View Raw Data →" button to see the full detail table.
🗂️
Module 2 — Raw Data
View and explore the underlying school data records
1
Navigate to Raw Data
Click 🗂️ Raw Data in the left sidebar under Main. This opens the full data table for all school years.
2
Use the Search Bar
Type in the search box to filter records by school year or any data value. Results update instantly across all data columns.
3
Read the Columns
Each row is one school year. Columns include: S.Y., G11 Enrolled, G12 Enrolled, Total, Repeaters, Dropouts, Teachers, Classrooms, plus computed KPI columns like dropout rate and utilization %.
4
Switch Between Tab Views
Use the tab buttons at the top of the page to toggle between All Data, Grade 11, Grade 12, At-Risk, and Resources views for focused data exploration.
💡 Tip: Raw Data is read-only. To update data, coordinate with your school ICT admin who manages the underlying data source.
💡
Module 3 — Insights
AI-generated analysis of enrollment patterns and school performance
1
Open the Insights Page
Click 💡 Insights in the sidebar under Analytics. The page loads pre-computed insights derived from all 6 years of school data.
2
Read the Insight Cards
Each card covers one finding — such as enrollment trends, dropout patterns, or classroom efficiency. Cards are color-coded: 🟢 Positive, 🔴 Alert, 🟡 Caution.
3
Check the Strengths & Risks Summary
At the bottom of the page you'll find two panels — School Strengths and Risk Factors — summarizing the overall health of the school at a glance.
🎯
Module 4 — Recommendations
Actionable next steps for school administration
1
Open Recommendations
Click 🎯 Recommendations in the sidebar. This page lists data-driven action items generated from the school's KPI analysis.
2
Understand the Priority Levels
Each recommendation is tagged with a priority level: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW. Start with high-priority items as they address the most critical risks.
3
Read the Action Steps
Each card includes a title, context explanation, and specific action steps that teachers and administrators can implement. Use these during planning meetings or LIS reviews.
🌱
Module 5 — Growth Opportunities
Identify areas where the school can expand and improve
1
Open Growth Opportunities
Click 🌱 Growth Opportunities in the sidebar under Analytics. This page highlights strategic areas for improvement based on enrollment and resource data.
2
Review Opportunity Cards
Each card describes a specific growth area — like increasing Grade 11 intake, reducing repeater rates, or optimizing classroom use — with estimated impact levels.
⚙️ Advanced Tools
⚙️
About the Advanced Tools
The four advanced tools let you run forward-looking simulations and action plans directly from your school's data. All inputs are pre-filled with the latest S.Y. 2025–2026 figures — simply adjust as needed and click the generate/calculate button to get results.
📈
Advanced Tool 1 — Enrollment Forecaster
Project future enrollment trends using historical data and adjustable growth scenarios
1
Open the Forecaster
Click 📈 Enrollment Forecaster in the sidebar under Advanced Tools. The page shows three input fields pre-filled with current data: Base Year Enrollment (319), Growth Scenario %/yr, and Forecast Years (1–10).
2
Set Your Scenario Inputs
Adjust the Growth Scenario field to model different futures — enter a positive value for growth (e.g., +3%/yr) or a negative value for a decline scenario (e.g., −5%/yr). Set how many school years ahead to project (up to 10). Then click 📈 Generate Forecast.
3
Read the Results
The tool displays a trajectory chart (solid line = historical, dashed = projected) and a detailed table showing projected enrollment, year-over-year change, recommended number of sections, and rooms needed per school year. An auto-generated insight summary appears below the table.
📌 Base Enrollment: 319 (S.Y. 2025–26)
📌 Growth Range: −20% to +30%/yr
📌 Forecast Range: 1–10 years
⚠️ Note: Projections use a compound growth model applied to the base year figure. External factors (policy changes, community events, economic conditions) are not modeled. Use results as planning guides, not guarantees.
🧑‍🏫
Advanced Tool 2 — Resource Allocator
Optimally allocate teachers and classrooms across strands and grade levels
1
Open the Resource Allocator
Click 🧑‍🏫 Resource Allocator in the sidebar. The tool loads with five editable input fields pre-filled with current school data: Total Students (319), Available Teachers (27), Available Classrooms (14), Target STR (25:1), and Max Students per Section (40).
2
Adjust Inputs & Calculate
Modify any input to model a scenario — for example, change Total Students to a forecasted enrollment figure, or adjust the Target STR to meet a stricter DepEd standard. Click 🧑‍🏫 Calculate Allocation to run the model.
3
Review the Allocation Output
Results include KPI summary cards (sections needed, teachers required, rooms required), a detailed allocation breakdown table, and an auto-generated insight. Surplus or deficit figures are highlighted — use these when preparing budget proposals or DepEd Division resource requests.
📌 Current STR: 319 ÷ 27 = 11.8:1
📌 DepEd Ideal STR: 18–25:1
📌 Section size: up to 60 students
🛡️
Advanced Tool 3 — Intervention Planner
Identify at-risk students early and generate a targeted intervention action plan
1
Open the Intervention Planner
Click 🛡️ Intervention Planner in the sidebar. The page loads with three input fields pre-filled from current data: Total Enrolled (319), Current Dropouts (9), and Current Repeaters. These values reflect S.Y. 2025–2026 actuals.
2
Enter Data & Generate the Plan
Update the dropout and repeater counts if needed (e.g., for a mid-year check), then click the generate button. The tool calculates the at-risk rate and produces a tiered action plan with priority levels, responsible personnel, and suggested timelines.
3
Review the Action Plan
The output separates students by risk category — dropout risk vs. academic repeaters — and recommends specific strategies such as peer tutoring, guidance counseling referrals, CCT follow-ups, and parent engagement activities. Use this as the basis for your school's official intervention documentation.
📌 At-Risk Count = Dropouts + Repeaters
📌 Dropout Rate target: < 3%
📌 Current dropout rate: 2.30%
🏫
Advanced Tool 4 — Utilization Simulator
Simulate classroom utilization rates under different enrollment and room scenarios
1
Open the Utilization Simulator
Click 🏫 Utilization Simulator in the sidebar. Three input fields appear pre-filled: Number of Classrooms (14), Seat Capacity per Room (40), and Current Enrollment (319). This tool answers "what-if" questions — e.g., "If enrollment rises to 380, are 14 rooms still enough?"
2
Adjust & Simulate
Change any of the three inputs to model your scenario — increase enrollment to a forecasted figure, reduce classroom count to reflect rooms under repair, or raise seat capacity for a policy change. Click the simulate button to instantly recalculate.
3
Interpret the Utilization Status
Results include the utilization percentage and a color-coded status indicator. Use this output to support room repurposing decisions, justify construction or repair requests to DepEd Division, or plan section expansion.
🟢
Optimal
75% – 100%
🟡
Under-utilized
Below 60%
🔴
Overcrowded
Above 110%
💡 Tip: Current utilization at 319 students ÷ (14 rooms × 40 seats) = ~57% — classified as under-utilized. This indicates available capacity for enrollment growth or room repurposing without construction.
🔐
Account — Security
Manage your session and account access
1
Open Security
Click 🔐 Security in the sidebar under Account, or access it via your profile dropdown in the top-right corner of any page.
2
Review Session Info
The Security Center shows your current session status, login time, device, and IP address. All access is logged for audit purposes.
3
Sign Out Properly
Always click Sign Out (in the sidebar footer or profile dropdown) when done — especially on shared computers. This clears your session and protects school data.
🌟
Tips & Frequently Asked Questions
Quick answers to common questions
❓ How do I navigate between pages?
Use the left sidebar on desktop. On mobile, tap the ☰ hamburger button in the top-left to open the menu, or use the bottom navigation bar for quick access to key pages.
❓ The charts are not showing. What do I do?
Try refreshing the page. Make sure you have a stable internet connection as the system loads Chart.js from a CDN. If the issue persists, try a different browser (Chrome recommended).
❓ Where does the data come from?
Data is sourced from official DepEd Learner Information System (LIS) records for SHS Magalang Stand Alone II, covering S.Y. 2020–2021 through S.Y. 2025–2026.
❓ Can I print or export the reports?
Use your browser's built-in Print function (Ctrl+P / Cmd+P) on any page to print or save as PDF. Charts and tables are optimized for print layout.
❓ Who do I contact for support?
Contact your school ICT coordinator or the dashboard developer. For DepEd-related data corrections, coordinate with your Schools Division Office (SDO) LIS coordinator.

🛡️ Security Recommendations
🔑
Change Passwords Regularly
Update your dashboard password every 90 days and avoid reusing old passwords.
🚪
Always Sign Out
Sign out after every session, especially on shared or public devices to prevent unauthorized access.
👁️
Monitor Login History
Review this page regularly. Report unfamiliar login times to your school administrator immediately.
🔒
Do Not Share Credentials
Each user role (Admin, Registrar, Teacher) should use their own credentials. Never share passwords.
📱
Secure Your Device
Use a screen lock on devices with dashboard access. Keep browsers and operating systems updated.
🌐
Use Trusted Networks
Avoid accessing the dashboard on public Wi-Fi without a VPN. Use the school's secure network when possible.
📋 New School Year Record
Enter the data for the new school year. YoY%, STR, Dropout Rate, and Utilization are auto-calculated from what you enter.
